description: This figure integrates transmission electron microscopy, nanoscale elemental mapping, and mechanical data to demonstrate Si-regulated formation of κ-carbides in homogenized austenitic Fe–30Mn–9Al–1.2C steel. The leftmost TEM micrograph (50 nm scale bar) reveals the nanoscale precipitate morphology. The second panel shows an elemental distribution map (20 nm scale bar) overlaid for Fe, Al, Si, and C, where carbon-rich regions identify the κ-carbides. The central engineering stress–strain curves compare 0Si, 1Si, and 2Si alloys: increasing Si raises the yield strength from approximately 450 MPa to 950 MPa, while tensile ductility drops from roughly 80% to about 20% engineering strain. The rightmost nanoscale distribution map (25 nm scale bar) exhibits additional elemental contrast consistent with carbon-enriched carbide regions. Collectively, the panels establish that Si addition promotes intragranular cuboidal κ-carbides (L′1, average 11.5 nm, 25.9 vol% at 2 mass% Si) and larger intergranular κ-carbides (50–200 nm), delivering substantial precipitation strengthening at the expense of reduced uniform elongation.
